data.description: A scale bar that shows real-world distance for a representative screen span at the current zoom level. Updates on camera move. Styled per RP-6. Should reflect Martian surface km (Mars radius ~3,390 km). Fixed bottom-right (or wherever it doesn't conflict with other controls).
data.title: Distance reference bar (scale bar)

data.body:
VISUAL SPEC FROM MOCKUPS (docs/mockups/LOAD SCREEN.png, ZOOM SCREEN.png):

POSITION: Bottom-center.

APPEARANCE: Bracket-style ruler — |————| with km label (e.g. '500 km') centered above the bar. White stroke and text against dark background. Minimal — no background card/panel.

DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR (confirmed by mockups):
  - LOAD SCREEN (globe fully visible): '500 km'
  - ZOOM SCREEN (zoomed in): '50 km'
  Bar width stays constant on screen; label and actual represented distance update with camera altitude.

IMPLEMENTATION: Compute from camera altitude + globe radius. At altitude h, visible angle θ, derive km/px. Pick a round number target (500, 200, 100, 50, 20, 10...) and set bar to that pixel width.

Mars radius: 3,390 km (use this for calculation, not Earth's).
